page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports hardware-assisted technologies such as AVX-512, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For local storage, the r6idn.12xlarge includes 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store capacity. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps, which also optimizes Amazon Elastic Block Store connectivity. This combination of high-speed local storage and high network throughput supports data-intensive operations without external storage bottlenecks.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates balanced performance. It achieves a Passmark CPU encryption score of 43605.77 and a Geekbench image processing score of 15409.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show 164.84 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 70.04 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Redis SET operations reach 44.3 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 42.15 GB/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of DDR4 memory paired with local NVMe storage provides a consolidated resource profile that reduces the need for multi-instance clustering for memory-bound workloads.

The r6idn.12xlarge is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is suitable for hosting large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving. Additionally, the CPU vector extensions and memory capacity support moderate-scale language model inference, processing 7B parameter models at 25.02 tokens per second for text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized, network-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. In benchmark testing, it exhibits strong performance in CPU encryption and image processing, alongside a Redis throughput of 44.3 million SET operations per second. With its high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage, the r6idn.12xlarge is highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for databases and high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized infrastructure solution featuring high-speed local NVMe storage, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and high-bandwidth networking for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core
2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong benchmark performance in CPU encryption and image processing
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and high-throughput web serving
